title: Themes
requirements: A Wii with BootMii installed (IOS or boot2)
An SD card, any size up to 32GB (formatted to FAT32)
A Wii Remote and sensor bar
An internet connection on the Wii
A computer with internet access (for building themes)
warnings: !!BootMii and Priiloader are REQUIRED!! — a corrupted custom theme can brick your System Menu without brick protection.
!!Only use .csm files built for your exact System Menu version and region!! (e.g. 4.3U, 4.3E, 4.3J). A version mismatch will brick.
!!Make a NAND backup!! before theming if you haven't already. See the str2hax guide if you skipped it.
_note: Themes customize the look of the Wii Menu — backgrounds, channel icons, sound effects, and fonts. They're distributed as .csm files. This guide uses csm-installer to apply them safely.

steps.title: Before you start
steps.body:
This guide installs a custom theme on your Wii Menu using **csm-installer**. Themes change the look and feel of the System Menu — backgrounds, icons, sounds, and more.

!!BIG WARNING!! — !!BootMii and Priiloader MUST be installed before theming!!. A corrupt or mismatched theme can brick your System Menu. Brick protection is your only way out. If you haven't installed them yet, follow the **str2hax** guide first.

First, I'll check that your SD card is formatted as FAT32 — csm-installer won't read anything else.

steps.title: Download csm-installer to your SD card
steps.body:
Graphite downloads **csm-installer** from the Open Shop Channel and unpacks it into the right `apps` folder on your SD card. This is the tool that installs the theme on your Wii.

Press the button and wait for it to finish.

steps.title: Download a base theme
steps.body:
csm-installer needs a clean, unmodified base theme from Nintendo's servers to compare against when installing your custom theme. You have two options:

**Option 1 — Download directly on the Wii (recommended):**
1. Put the SD card in your Wii and power it on.
2. Launch the **Homebrew Channel** and open **csm-installer**.
3. Select **Download base** (or press 1 on your Wii Remote).
4. csm-installer automatically downloads the correct base for your System Menu version and region.
5. Press **B** to go back, then exit to the System Menu and power off.
6. Move the SD card back to your computer.

**Option 2 — Download on your PC:**
Use **NUSGet** (or NUS Downloader) to grab the System Menu WAD for your region (e.g. `0000000100000002` for 4.3). Keep the downloaded `.app` file — you'll point ThemeMii Mod to it in a later step.

Either way, you need this base before building any custom theme.

steps.title: Find a .mym theme
steps.body:
Themes are created as `.mym` files — a format used by **ThemeMii Mod** to store custom assets. Here's where to find them:

- **Wii Theme Team Creations** — `gbatemp.net/threads/wii-theme-team-creations.468678/` — huge curated collection
- **GBAtemp Themes section** — browse the Wii subsection for user-made themes
- **Wii Themer** — third-party site with searchable theme database

Download a `.mym` file you like. Keep it somewhere easy to find — you'll use it in the next step.

!!Check the theme's description!! — it should specify which System Menu version and region it's made for (e.g. 4.3U). Using the wrong version will brick.

steps.title: Build a .csm from your .mym
steps.body:
Your `.mym` file needs to be converted into a `.csm` file that the Wii can install. **ThemeMii Mod** does this on your PC.

1. Download **ThemeMii Mod** and open it.
2. Click **File** → **Open** and select your `.mym` theme file.
3. Click **File** → **Open Base** and point it to the base theme you downloaded earlier (the `.app` from NUSGet, or the base csm-installer saved to your SD).
4. Click **Create Csm**. Choose where to save it.
5. Name it something you'll recognize (e.g. `dark-theme.csm`) and copy it onto your SD card.

If you downloaded the base directly on the Wii via csm-installer, the base is already on your SD card in the `csm-installer` folder. Point ThemeMii Mod to that.

!!Make sure the base matches your Wii's System Menu version!!

troubleshoot.label: ThemeMii Mod won't open or crashes

steps.title: Clean up and eject the card
steps.body:
macOS scatters hidden junk files (`.DS_Store`, `._` files) onto SD cards, and Wii homebrew can choke on them.

Press the button, and Graphite strips those files off the card and **safely ejects it for you**. Once it pops off, pull the card out.

steps.title: Install the theme on your Wii
steps.body:
1. Insert the SD card into the **SD card slot on the front of the Wii**.
2. Power on the Wii.
3. Launch the **Homebrew Channel**.
4. Open **csm-installer**.
5. csm-installer scans your SD card for `.csm` files. Select your theme from the list.
6. Press the **+ Button** on your Wii Remote (or **A** on a GameCube controller) to confirm.
7. Wait for the install to finish — csm-installer patches the theme against the base and installs it. This takes a few seconds.
8. When done, press **A** to return to csm-installer, then press **HOME** on your Wii Remote and select **Exit to System Menu**.

The Wii Menu reloads with your new theme applied.

troubleshoot.label: The theme didn't install or looks wrong

steps.title: You're done
steps.body:
Your Wii Menu is now themed! If you want a different look, repeat these steps with another `.mym` file — just re-build the `.csm` and install it.

If the theme ever causes issues, hold **RESET** while powering on to enter **Priiloader**. From there you can launch the **Homebrew Channel** and re-install a different theme, or restore your NAND backup.

To remove a theme and go back to stock, use csm-installer's **Uninstall theme** option.
